title: Chad Tracy provides injury updates for Garrett Crochet, Roman Anthony
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Chad Tracy provided injury updates for Garrett Crochet and Roman Anthony, both lacking a clear return timetable.
Crochet will throw this weekend ahead of a live batting practice session planned for next week.
Roman Anthony's pain issues persist after a swing attempt, complicating his return from a sprain.

title: Sam Kennedy says Red Sox looking to add bat ‘earlier than ever’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox President Sam Kennedy is exploring trade options to boost the team's offensive lineup early in the season.
Boston ranks low in key offensive stats, including second-least in runs (181) and home runs (36) in MLB.
Red Sox have been in talks with various teams, aiming to secure an MLB-proven hitter to improve playoff chances.

title: Mock draft predicts Red Sox will select Bishop Feehan star in first round
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: MLB.com's Jonathan Mayo predicts Red Sox will draft Bishop Feehan's Brody Bumila at No. 20 in June.
The 6-foot-9 lefty, Brody Bumila, features a fastball-heavy approach with a low three-quarter arm slot delivery.
While Bumila's fastball is impressive, concerns about his secondary pitches and previous UCL injury might affect his draft ranking.

title: Red Sox’ Trevor Story undergoes sports hernia surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox shortstop Trevor Story underwent hernia surgery and could miss 6-10 weeks recovering.
Story's stint with the Red Sox has been marred by injuries, including shoulder and elbow surgeries.
Marcelo Mayer may make his MLB shortstop debut Sunday, filling in during Story's absence.

title: Peter Wolf left one Boston art school to form the J. Geils Band. Six decades later, he earned an honorary doctorate from another.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Peter Wolf, former frontman of the J. Geils Band, received an honorary doctorate from Massachusetts College of Art and Design.
Wolf, who attended but didn't graduate from Boston Museum School of Fine Arts, left to pursue a music career.
Wolf's memoir "Waiting on the Moon" shares stories of meeting famous figures during his diverse artistic journey.

title: Boston 25 hires Lauren Walsh for newly-created role
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Lauren Walsh, a Medford native, joins Boston 25 as a sports media journalist in a newly-created role.
Walsh spent the past three years at WSMV in Nashville and previously worked in Winston-Salem.
A Syracuse graduate, Walsh has previously worked as a sports anchor in Burlington, Vt. and Syracuse, N.Y.

title: Somerville restaurant owner says viral ‘pool water’ reply to customer review was unauthorized
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Owner Simona Sbano of Olivia's Kitchen claims an unauthorized reply to a customer review doesn't reflect the restaurant's values.
The controversial reply criticized Somerville tap water, leading Olivia's Kitchen to change its policy and serve tap water upon request.
Following the incident, Olivia's Kitchen disciplined the responsible employee, who was eventually let go due to subsequent issues.

title: With split vote, City Council effort to reject Wu’s budget fails
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council's motion to reject Mayor Wu's budget proposal failed with a 6-6 tie vote.
Councilors face a deadline of June 10 to accept, reject, or amend Wu's proposed fiscal budget.
Mayor Wu's budget proposal includes $70 million in reserve funds to address city and school deficits.

title: Kevin McHale questions Celtics’ 3-point heavy approach: ‘The ball has got to touch the paint’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kevin McHale criticizes Celtics' reliance on 3-pointers, suggesting they need to attack the paint more.
The Celtics led in 3-point attempts but struggled with points in the paint and free-throw opportunities.
Brad Stevens acknowledges need for shot variance, highlighting potential team additions to achieve it.

title: Mass. pols issue report on possible effects of Haiti TPS termination on local health care
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers issued a report warning that ending Haitian TPS may worsen healthcare worker shortages and endanger patient care.
The report noted Massachusetts would face severe impacts with 45,000 residents relying on TPS, many in healthcare jobs.
Healthcare providers anticipate service gaps, increased wait times, and staffing issues if Haitian TPS is terminated.
